title: [fixed] CLAUDE.md: "Three modules" framing is misleading
description: Adversarial review finding #1 (critical). CLAUDE.md said "Three modules under src/" but listed five items (3 directories + 2 shared files). Fixed: changed to "Three directories under src/, plus two shared root-level files". File: CLAUDE.md. See docs/adversarial-review.md §1.

title: [fixed] README: --bounds warning promoted to callout
description: Adversarial review finding #2 (critical). The --bounds flag uses a non-standard south,north,west,east format. The old parenthetical warning was insufficient — users familiar with WGS84 (west,south,east,north) or Google (south,west,north,east) conventions will silently get wrong results. Fixed: promoted to a > **Warning** callout block in README.md. See docs/adversarial-review.md §2.

title: Consider adopting WGS84 --bounds convention (south,north,west,east → west,south,east,north)
description:
Follow-up from adversarial review finding #2. The --bounds flag currently uses south,north,west,east — a format unique to this codebase. Every standard geographic tool uses west,south,east,north (WGS84/GeoJSON) or south,west,north,east (Google). Changing the format would prevent silent errors for anyone familiar with geographic tooling.

Files to modify:
- src/pipeline/extract-dump.ts (parseBounds function)
- src/pipeline/build.ts (--bounds arg parsing)
- README.md and docs/data-extraction.md (update examples)
- All tests referencing --bounds

This is a breaking change to the CLI. Consider adding a deprecation warning for the old format, or just change it since the tool is pre-1.0.

title: [fixed] architecture.md: Added tile deserialization failure to failure modes table
description: Adversarial review finding #3 (critical). The failure modes table in docs/architecture.md had no entry for binary deserialization failures (corrupt .bin files, truncated downloads, format version mismatch). Fixed: added row documenting that deserializeBinary() throws on corrupt data, caught by tile-loader, error logged, tile skipped. See docs/adversarial-review.md §3.

title: Add defensive validation to deserializeBinary() for corrupt/truncated tiles
description:
Follow-up from adversarial review finding #3. The deserializeBinary() function in src/geometry/serialization.ts reads raw bytes with offset arithmetic. A malformed buffer (truncated mobile download, bit-flip, future format mismatch) will either throw a RangeError or produce silent garbage.

Suggested approach:
1. Validate the magic bytes at offset 0 (currently 0x44454C41 = 'DELA')
2. Check that buffer length >= header size (24 bytes) before reading header fields
3. Validate that V (vertex count) and T (triangle count) produce a total size matching buffer.byteLength
4. Wrap the entire function in a try-catch that returns a typed error rather than letting RangeError propagate

Files: src/geometry/serialization.ts (deserializeBinary function), add test cases in src/geometry/serialization.test.ts for truncated, zero-length, and garbage buffers.

The failure modes table in docs/architecture.md already documents the expected behavior (throw → caught → tile skipped). This task ensures the code actually matches that documentation.

title: [fixed] binary-format.md: Documented IDB key prefix migration strategy for reserved header bytes
description: Adversarial review finding #4 (critical). The binary format has 8 reserved bytes (16-23) with a vague "will be placed here if layout changes" note but no actual migration strategy. Fixed: documented that format migration is handled by bumping the IDB key prefix (e.g. tile-v1- → tile-v2-), which orphans old cached tiles. See docs/adversarial-review.md §4.

title: Add format version field to binary header reserved bytes 16-23
description:
Follow-up from adversarial review finding #4. The binary format (docs/binary-format.md) has 8 reserved bytes at offset 16-23 that are currently zero-filled. The IDB key prefix migration strategy is now documented, but a version field in the header itself would allow the deserializer to detect mismatches and fail gracefully instead of producing garbage.

Suggested approach:
1. Write a uint32 version number (value: 1) at offset 16 in serializeBinary() (src/geometry/serialization.ts)
2. Read and validate it in deserializeBinary() — throw a clear error like 'Unsupported binary format version: N' if it doesn't match
3. Update docs/binary-format.md to reflect the new field layout
4. Remaining 4 bytes at offset 20-23 stay reserved
5. Existing tiles in the wild have 0x00000000 at offset 16 — decide whether version 0 = version 1 (lenient) or requires re-pipeline (strict)

Files: src/geometry/serialization.ts, src/geometry/serialization.test.ts, docs/binary-format.md.

title: [fixed] tiling.md: Clarified average article count includes buffer overlap
description: Adversarial review finding #5 (moderate). The back-of-envelope math table showed "Average articles per tile: ~1,500" but 1M articles / 800 tiles = 1,250. The 1,500 figure includes buffer zone duplicates. Fixed: changed label to "Average articles per tile (incl. buffer)". See docs/adversarial-review.md §5.

title: [fixed] nearest-neighbor.md: Corrected complexity claim from "randomized" to deterministic
description: Adversarial review finding #6 (moderate). The O(N log N) claim said "randomized incremental hull" but the implementation uses deterministic insertion order. Fixed: changed to "O(N log N) for typical geographic distributions" with a note that the bound relies on input distribution, not algorithmic randomization. See docs/adversarial-review.md §6.

title: Document CI vs local lint divergence in CLAUDE.md
description:
Adversarial review finding #7 (moderate). The local npm run lint command runs tsc + eslint + prettier sequentially, but CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml) splits these into three parallel jobs: lint (Prettier + ESLint), type-check (tsc), test (vitest). This means CI can pass when local lint fails or vice versa (e.g. tsc fails in a different environment). The docs don't mention this divergence.

Fix: Add a note to CLAUDE.md's Commands section explaining that npm run lint is a superset of what CI runs. Something like: "Note: CI splits this into parallel jobs (lint, type-check, test). npm run lint runs all three sequentially — if it passes locally, CI will pass."

Files: CLAUDE.md (Commands section, near line 11).

title: [fixed] architecture.md: Renamed "Key Files" to "Key Files (Selected)"
description: Adversarial review finding #8 (moderate). The Key Files section presented itself as comprehensive but was missing src/app/format.ts, src/tiles.ts exports, and detailed type descriptions. Fixed: renamed to "Key Files (Selected)" to drop the implicit claim of completeness. See docs/adversarial-review.md §8.

title: Complete architecture.md Key Files section with missing entries
description:
Follow-up from adversarial review finding #8. The "Key Files (Selected)" section in docs/architecture.md is missing several important files and export details:

1. src/app/format.ts — exports directionsUrl(), wikipediaUrl(), formatDistance() for distance formatting and URL construction
2. src/app/types.ts — listed but exports (Article, NearbyArticle, UserPosition) aren't described
3. src/tiles.ts — not listed; exports GRID_DEG, BUFFER_DEG, EDGE_PROXIMITY_DEG constants and tileId(), tilesForBounds() functions used by both pipeline and app
4. src/pipeline/canary.ts — listed but the LANDMARKS record structure (Record<string, {lat, lon}>) isn't described

Either add these entries with brief export descriptions, or add a note saying "For a complete file listing, see the source tree directly."

File: docs/architecture.md, "Key Files (Selected)" section (around line 240).

title: [fixed] CLAUDE.md and README: Added memory requirement note for extract command
description: Adversarial review finding #9 (moderate). The npm run extract command silently sets --max-old-space-size=6144 (6 GB heap), but this was only documented in docs/data-extraction.md, not in CLAUDE.md or README.md where users actually run the command. Fixed: added note to both files. See docs/adversarial-review.md §9.

title: [fixed] README: Documented BASE_URL / vite base path dependency for forks
description: Adversarial review finding #10 (moderate). The app uses import.meta.env.BASE_URL (configured via base: "/tour-guide/" in vite.config.ts) for tile fetch URLs. Forks deploying to a different path would get silent tile loading failures. Fixed: added deployment note to README. See docs/adversarial-review.md §10.

title: Expand innerHTML ESLint ban to cover insertAdjacentHTML and outerHTML
description:
Adversarial review finding #11 (minor). The ESLint no-restricted-syntax rule in eslint.config.mjs (lines 26-33) bans el.innerHTML = x but does NOT catch el.insertAdjacentHTML(), document.write(), or el.outerHTML = x. The docs/architecture.md claim "innerHTML banned" implies a stronger XSS guarantee than actually exists.

Fix options:
1. Add ESLint selectors for insertAdjacentHTML and outerHTML assignments to eslint.config.mjs
2. Add a document.write ban (unlikely to be used but completes the surface)
3. Update the architecture.md claim to be precise: "innerHTML assignment banned" rather than implying all dangerous DOM mutation is covered

Files: eslint.config.mjs (no-restricted-syntax rules), docs/architecture.md (security section around line 138).

title: Replace ASCII state machine diagram with Mermaid or keep in sync
description:
Adversarial review finding #12 (minor). The ASCII transition diagram in docs/state-machine.md (lines 101-124) uses box-drawing characters and precise spacing. Any edit to surrounding text or a font change will misalign it. Updating ASCII art is painful enough that people skip it, so the diagram will drift from reality silently.

Options:
1. Replace with a Mermaid stateDiagram-v2 block (GitHub renders Mermaid natively in markdown). This is self-maintaining since the syntax is semantic, not visual.
2. Keep ASCII but add a test that validates the diagram matches the actual state machine transitions defined in src/app/state-machine.ts
3. Accept the maintenance burden and document it as a known tradeoff

The state machine has states: boot, locating, loading, ready, detail, error. Transitions are defined in src/app/state-machine.ts.

File: docs/state-machine.md (lines 101-124).

title: Revise "Why not S2 cells" rationale in tiling.md to be more direct
description:
Adversarial review finding #13 (minor). The docs/tiling.md "Why not S2 cells" section argues that "computing S2 cell IDs from lat/lon requires implementing the S2 projection (a non-trivial amount of code)" and cites the zero-dependency constraint. But the project already vendors robust-predicates (~400 lines), so ~200 lines of S2 math would be precedented. The real reason is probably that the 5° grid is good enough and simpler.

Fix: Rewrite the rationale to lead with the practical argument ("The 5° grid is simple, fast to compute, and adequate for our use case") and mention S2 as a theoretically better but unnecessary alternative. Keep the zero-dependency note but acknowledge the vendoring precedent.

File: docs/tiling.md (lines 27-28, "Why not S2 cells" section).

title: Automate README article counts or remove hardcoded numbers
description:
Adversarial review finding #14 (minor). README.md (lines 164-168) hardcodes article counts: English ~1.2M, Swedish ~250K, Japanese ~180K with the note "Counts are approximate and update monthly via the automated pipeline." But the automated pipeline updates tile data, not the README. These counts will drift from reality.

Options:
1. Add a pipeline post-step that updates README.md article counts from the actual extracted data (data/articles-{lang}.json line counts)
2. Remove the specific numbers and say "see the latest tile index for current counts"
3. Accept drift and change the note to "Counts are approximate as of [date]" without implying auto-update

Files: README.md (article counts section), optionally src/pipeline/build.ts or a new post-pipeline script.

title: Add code comment linking Workbox cache config to architecture.md
description:
Adversarial review finding #15 (minor). The Wikipedia API cache settings (max 200 entries, 1-week expiry) are configured in vite.config.ts via Workbox runtimeCaching but documented in docs/architecture.md. If someone modifies the Vite config without reading the architecture docs, the values will fall out of sync.

Fix: Add a comment in vite.config.ts near the Workbox runtimeCaching config that says something like: "// Cache policy documented in docs/architecture.md — keep in sync". This is a 1-line change.

File: vite.config.ts (Workbox runtimeCaching section).

title: Add documentation reading-order guide to README
description:
Adversarial review structural observation. The six docs/ files are individually excellent but there's no unified entry point that says "read these in this order." A reader landing on binary-format.md has no context for why the format exists.

Fix: Add a reading-order recommendation in the README's Documentation section. Suggested order:
1. docs/architecture.md — system overview, data flow, key files
2. docs/data-extraction.md — Phase 1: getting data from Wikipedia
3. docs/tiling.md — Phase 2: geographic partitioning strategy
4. docs/binary-format.md — Phase 2: serialization format
5. docs/nearest-neighbor.md — Phase 3: query algorithm
6. docs/state-machine.md — Phase 3: app UI states

File: README.md (Documentation section).

title: Add audience note to CLAUDE.md (AI agents vs human developers)
description:
Adversarial review structural observation. CLAUDE.md is clearly written for AI coding assistants — the session completion protocol, bd issue tracking commands, and TodoWrite mentions read like a checklist for a robot, not workflow guidance for a human developer. This is fine if the intended audience is AI agents, but the file should probably say so explicitly.

Fix: Add a brief note near the top of CLAUDE.md, e.g.: "This file provides instructions for AI coding assistants (Claude Code, etc.) working in this repository. Human developers should refer to README.md for getting started."

File: CLAUDE.md (near the top, after the title).
